ALL SPECIALTIES / SPEECH-LANGUAGE PATHOLOGIST / NEW YORK

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

New York Speech-Language Pathologist License

New York Speech-Language Pathologist license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-02-25 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Speech-Language Pathologist applications in New York run through State Board for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Official graduate transcripts (Form 2) - Certification of Professional Education from a CAA-accredited program. May incur an additional institutional fee.
  • Praxis exam score report - Score of 162 or higher on test 5331. Sent directly to NYSED using agency code R7747.
  • Supervised experience forms (4A/4B) - Verification of 36 weeks of supervised professional experience signed by supervisor.
  • Child abuse and harassment training - Completion certificates for mandated New York coursework in child abuse identification and harassment prevention.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 6 to 8 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

New York does not have formal reciprocity agreements or participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for full New York licensure and meet all state-specific requirements. ASHA CCC-SLP holders must still submit all required forms and documentation to NYSED.

  1. STEP 1

    Meet Education Requirements

    Complete a Master's or Doctor's degree in Speech-Language Pathology from a program registered by the NYSED or determined by the Department to be equivalent. This education must include at least 400 clock hours of supervised clinical practicum.

    Fee: $294 · Timeline: 6 to 8 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Pass Praxis Examination

    Achieve a passing score on the Praxis Subject Assessment in Speech-Language Pathology (Code 5031) administered by ETS. You must request ETS to send your official score report directly to the NYSED Office of the Professions.

    Fee: $294 · Timeline: 6 to 8 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Complete Supervised Experience

    Complete a supervised clinical experience, often referred to as a Clinical Fellowship Year (CFY), consisting of 1200 hours over a minimum of 36 weeks. This experience must be attested to on Form 4B by your supervisor and submitted directly to NYSED.

    Fee: $294 · Timeline: 6 to 8 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Submit Application and Fee

    Download and complete the 'Application for Licensure' (Form 1) from the NYSED Office of the Professions website. Include the required application fee of $294. Mail the complete form and fee to the Office of the Professions.

    Fee: $294 · Timeline: 6 to 8 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Submit Supporting Documentation

    Arrange for your college or university to send official transcripts, verifying your degree and supervised practicum hours, directly to NYSED using 'Certification of Professional Education' (Form 2). Ensure your supervisor submits 'Verification of Supervised Experience' (Form 4B) directly to NYSED.

    Fee: $294 · Timeline: 6 to 8 Weeks

  6. STEP 6

    Await License Issuance

    The NYSED Office of the Professions will review your complete application and all supporting documents. Allow adequate time for processing; you will be notified of your license status upon completion of the review.

    Fee: $294 · Timeline: 6 to 8 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$294
Background check
$0 (included in application)
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$294

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Board
State Board for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
Phone
(518) 474-3817 ext. 570
Website
op.nysed.gov/prof/slpa/
Application page
Open application

Renewal

Speech-Language Pathologist renewal in New York follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 3 years
Renewal fee
$210
Continuing education
36 hours every three years

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does a New York SLP license cost?

The total initial licensure and registration fee is $294, submitted with Form 1 to NYSED. A Limited License for supervised practice costs an additional $70. Triennial renewal is $155.

Do I need a TSSLD to work in New York schools?

Yes. To work as an SLP in a New York school setting, you need the Teacher of Students with Speech and Language Disability (TSSLD) credential in addition to your state SLP license. The TSSLD is issued by NYSED and has separate application requirements.

How long does the New York SLP application take?

Processing time is approximately six to eight weeks once all documents are received by NYSED. Incomplete applications will cause significant delays. Submit all forms and fees together to avoid processing issues.

Does New York have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in New York. To work in New York, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to New York?

Most endorsement applications take 6 to 8 weeks.

Can I start working in New York while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$294

Processing time

6 to 8 Weeks

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Available · $70

National exam

National Examination in Speech-Language Pathology

Compact

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

Every 3 years · $210

Licensing board

State Board for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology

op.nysed.gov/prof/slpa/

(518) 474-3817 ext. 570

This page is maintained as a reference for speech-language pathologists and recruiters evaluating New York assignments.
